summary: The Goshen Township Board of Zoning Appeals held a public hearing on case BZA 2026-001, a request by property owner Dave Schooler for an area dimensional variance to increase the maximum height of a freestanding monument sign from 10 feet to 26 feet on his M-2 heavy manufacturing district property at 1495 State Route 28. The property is accessed only via a private drive with no direct frontage on State Route 28, making visibility a challenge. After extensive discussion among board members, the applicant, and neighboring property owner Nick Lefty, the board unanimously denied the variance request 5-0, finding it substantial, not the minimum necessary, and in conflict with Article 13.09 sign standards. New board member Clarence Groves was sworn in at the beginning of the meeting.
decisions: Minutes from January 12th meeting approved (3 ayes, 2 abstentions)
Motion to recess for executive session discussion approved unanimously
Motion to continue case BZA 2026-001 to May 18th died for lack of second after recess
BZA case 2026-001 denied 5-0 - request to increase monument sign height from 10 feet to 26 feet on parcel 112208F115 at 1495 State Route 28
action_items: Dave Schooler may still install a standard 10-foot sign on his property without a variance
Dave Schooler and Nick Lefty discussed potentially collaborating on shared signage on State Route 28 frontage
Staff noted Dave Schooler is eligible for a wall sign on his existing building without a variance
BZA still seeking one alternate board member - interested parties should submit resumes
Zoning Commission also has an open spot available
key_amounts: 64 square feet proposed sign area (within 75 sq ft maximum allowed)
26 feet proposed sign height (vs 10 feet maximum permitted)
206 feet of calculated frontage on private drive
45 feet maximum building height in M2 district
10-foot sign setback from property line for non-residential
50-foot sign setback from residential property line
26 acres total property the applicant is trying to develop
notable_topics: Area dimensional variance request for monument sign height increase from 10 to 26 feet
Swearing in of new board member Clarence Groves
Property access limitations via private drive with no State Route 28 frontage
Impact of potential future development on former Combs dump site parcels
Discussion of alternative signage solutions including shared sign on State Route 28
Visibility concerns given property is approximately 1000 feet from State Route 28
Executive session recess to discuss compromise height options
Article 13 signage standards and Section 3.06 variance criteria analysis
attendees: Mr. Groves (Clarence Groves, new BZA member, sworn in)
Tammy Burkle (neighboring property owner, 1511 State Route 28, spoke in favor)
Nick Lefty (adjacent property owner, spoke as neutral/questioning)
notes: The board went into executive session/recess to discuss a potential compromise on sign height. During discussion, members considered heights of 18, 20, and 22 feet as alternatives to the requested 26 feet, but after recess the board ultimately denied the full variance request 5-0 rather than approving a reduced height. The applicant has owned the property since 1999 and has struggled with visibility. The former Combs dump site in front of his property was recently cleaned up and is expected to be rezoned to B-2 and developed, which would further obstruct visibility. No applications were received for the May 18th meeting so the next meeting will be in June. Meeting ran from 6:30 PM to 7:54 PM.
title: BZA 4-20-26
